closed_children.title: Drop per-worktree .balls/local symlink; consumers resolve XDG paths directly
Sunset pending_sync_legacy::warn_if_present (move diagnostic into bl doctor)
Relocate last_fetch marker from local_dir/ to ~/.cache/balls/<nested>/
Fold LocalConfig (config.json) + tasks_dir marker into clone.json

description:
Phase 1A (bl-203e) implemented XDG mode and invented a `state/<nested>/` catch-all bucket under `~/.local/state/balls/` to host files inherited from the pre-XDG `.balls/local/` layout. That bucket is **not in SPEC §3**, which names exactly these per-clone slots under `~/.local/state/balls/`:

> `trackers/<enc-origin>/<enc-balls-tasks>/` — state-repo checkout
> `worktrees/<nested-clone-path>/<bl-id>/` — task worktrees
> `claims/<nested-clone-path>/` — claim files (per clone)
> `locks/<nested-clone-path>/` — task locks (per clone)
> `plugins-auth/<nested-clone-path>/` — plugin tokens, identity (per clone, per user)

plus `~/.config/balls/<nested-clone-path>/clone.json` (§6.4) and the reserved `~/.cache/balls/` for derived artifacts. Nothing else.

This ball is the **final cleanup step** of Phase 1A's SPEC drift: it deletes the catch-all bucket and the `Store::local_dir()` accessor that points to it. It can only land after every consumer has been relocated to its SPEC-aligned home.

## History — original deps (each cleared one orphan)

- **bl-cb73** (archived) — squash-pid scratch replaced by `commit-tree` plumbing.
- **bl-1432** (archived) — `seen-claim-sync-policy` marker replaced by reactive emission in `bl claim`.
- **bl-6969** (archived) — pending-sync staging deferred entirely (kept `FailurePolicy::Gating` as schema-accepted degrade).

## New deps (filed 2026-05-26 after audit found 5 callers still live on main)

- **bl-5a03** — Fold `LocalConfig` (config.json) + `tasks_dir` marker into `clone.json` (SPEC §6.4 owns this).
- **bl-5814** — Relocate `last_fetch` marker to `~/.cache/balls/<nested>/` (SPEC §3 reserved slot).
- **bl-51a5** — Drop the per-worktree `.balls/local` symlink; rewrite consumers to use Store XDG accessors directly.
- **bl-341b** — Sunset `pending_sync_legacy::warn_if_present`; move the diagnostic into `bl doctor` reading the *legacy* `<repo>/.balls/local/pending-sync/` path.

## Scope (after all seven deps land)

1. Verify the accessor is dead: `git grep \"local_dir\" -- src/ tests/` returns only the accessor's own definition.
2. Delete `Store::local_dir()` and the `local_dir_path` field from `src/store.rs`.
3. Delete the `local_dir` field from `xdg_discover::XdgResolution`; delete the assignments in `xdg_discover::build_store` and `store_legacy::legacy_with`.
4. Delete `let local_dir = bases.state_root().join(\"state\").join(&nested)` from `xdg_discover::try_resolve` (non-stealth) and `xdg_discover::build_stealth` (stealth). No more `~/.local/state/balls/state/<nested>/` materialization.
5. Update `xdg_discover_tests` constructors that hand-build `XdgResolution`.

## No SPEC change

The `state/<nested>/` path was never in SPEC §3; deleting it brings the implementation into alignment with the SPEC as written. No deferral note needed — the path simply ceases to exist.

## What this ball does NOT do

- Does not add a new SPEC §3 row. The four new dep balls each route their orphan to an existing SPEC-named home.
- Does not touch `Store::stealth` / `Store::no_git` field semantics — those describe modes, not paths.
title: Retire Store::local_dir() and the state/<nested>/ catch-all — final step of Phase 1A SPEC §3 alignment
